7. `runtime-owner-8-mio-poller-owner-loop`

8. Post-poller gates:

## Completed Hot-Path Packet Family

The next auto selector wave is intentionally smaller than owner-owned DB. It is
documented in `docs/RUNTIME_OWNER_HOTPATH_PUSH.md` and targets three measured
post-poller costs:

1. `runtime-owner-10-hotpath-timing-gate`
   architect packet.
9. **Compatibility gate.** `runtime-owner-8-post-poller-oracle` must pass
   before any post-poller benchmark evidence is trusted.

## Post-Poller Hotpath Wave (locked 2026-05-22)

`runtime-owner-8` completed cleanly enough to make the next performance wall
local and measurable rather than structural. The owner-owned DB migration is
still the larger manual architecture decision, but it is not the first next
step. The current macOS call-tree artifacts show three smaller faithful-port
cost centers that can be tested directly with the existing harness runners:

1. **Command timing / slowlog predicate overhead.** `dispatch_command_name()`
   currently pays `Instant::now()` plus `elapsed()` on every command before
   deciding whether slowlog should record anything. Upstream Valkey also times
   command execution in `server.c::call()`, so this packet may reduce avoidable
   work but may not disable timing semantics, slowlog, latency stats, AOF, or
   replication.

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_119","type":"agent_message","text":"ARCHITECT DECISIONS:
- Owner-owned DB migration is staged: first route DB access through `CommandContext`, then remove `global_databases()` from cross-DB command paths, then flip `RuntimeOwner` to the single live `Vec<RedisDb>`.
- No second live keyspace is allowed: TLS, active expire, replication apply, AOF replay, and persistence entry must route through owner-owned effects after the flip.
- Evidence basis: `runtime-owner-12` oracle/profile/calltree and p100 regression gates passed; remaining work is ownership-correctness, not benchmark fast paths.
- TLS socket migration remains a human sequencing question, but TLS command effects may not stay on a divergent DB.

FILES UPDATED:
- `docs/RUNTIME_OWNER_NEXT_PUSH.md`: refreshed current status and next owner-owned DB packet family.
- `harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md`: locked owner-owned DB decisions, evidence, and stop conditions.
- `harness/architecture/lifecycle-map.toml`: added owner-owned DB lifecycle state, transitions, and invariants.
- `harness/work-packets.jsonl`: appended staged runtime-owner 13-15 packets and gates.
- `harness/completion.toml`: changed completion target to owner-owned DB migration and added new criteria.

PACKETS CREATED OR CHANGED:
- `runtime-owner-13-db-context-router`: translator, `CommandContext` selected/cross-DB routing groundwork.
- `runtime-owner-13-post-db-context-oracle`: runner, wire-smoke gate.
- `runtime-owner-14-cross-db-owner-routing`: translator, remove command-path `global_databases()` from MOVE/COPY/SWAPDB/EXEC/wake routing.
- `runtime-owner-14-post-cross-db-oracle`: runner, wire-smoke gate.
- `runtime-owner-15-owner-owned-db-loop`: translator, flip RuntimeOwner to owned live DB vector and route background/external effects.
- `runtime-owner-15-post-owned-db-*` and `runtime-owner-15-regression-*`: oracle, profile, calltree, and p100 regression gates.

HUMAN QUESTIONS:
- none new; existing TLS transport, I/O-thread parity, and soak-runner TODOs remain for public/production claim decisions.

NEXT SAFE DISPATCH:
- `runtime-owner-13-db-context-router` after this architect packet is recorded in the ledger."}}
